ENGL134 The Rites of Discovery: Science, Law, and Literature, 1492-1992 (3 Credits)

History of idea of "discovery" from sixteenth-century debate about European "rights of discovery” to 500th anniversary, in 1992, of Columbus’ landfall in New World. Evolution of modern concept of discovery, both

as part of history of science and in legal context of history of European colonialism and cultural encounter with Native peoples of Americas, Africa, and Asia. Exploration of primary and secondary sources relating to international law, science, and literature.

2025-2026 [updated 2025.04.17]
